intro: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE Abogados makes available to its clients, users of its website (registered or not), suppliers, advertisers, sponsors, associated professionals and business partners or associated entities, this PRIVACY POLICY, which shall be considered known and expressly accepted in all its terms by natural or legal persons who access and use the website www.cacciatoreabogados.com.ar and its related pages or websites.
intro2: This privacy policy is the exclusive property of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE, and may be found constantly and updated on our firm's website, in the LEGAL section, at the bottom of the page.
intro3: We want you to feel safe every time you visit our website and that is why we are committed to ensuring compliance with our privacy policies.
objeto: The purpose of this Privacy Policy is to inform our clients and/or any user and/or visitor of our website — whether or not registered — as well as our business partners, sponsors, advertisers, suppliers, associated professionals, and/or any other third party that interacts directly or indirectly with CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE, regarding the stipulations contemplated in this policy, in a clear, concrete and detailed manner, about the collection, use and processing of information and personal data that we may eventually collect or obtain through our website, in accordance with current regulations: Law 25.326 (Personal Data Protection Law), its regulatory decree number 1558/01, other complementary regulations and provisions issued by the Agency of Access to Public Information (Law 27.225), and its related provisions.
objeto2: Furthermore, this Privacy Policy forms an integral part of the Terms and Conditions of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE.
h_info: INFORMATION WE COLLECT AND HOW WE OBTAIN IT
info_intro: Basically, our website may collect data or information in two ways:
info_directa: 1) Direct: this occurs when site users provide information on various pages of our site or when they provide information through our contact form. In the latter case, said form has data or information fields such as: name, surname, email address, telephone, organization, area of legal practice that their message, query or inquiry will cover.
info_indirecta: 2) Indirect: this occurs through the technology of our website, which allows the user to connect with different platforms or networks available there currently or in the future, such as social networks: Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Zoom, YouTube, Eventbrite, Facebook Workplace, WhatsApp, Google My Business, Google Maps and Gmail. Each of these platforms has its own privacy policy, its own cookie policy and its own terms and conditions; documents and regulations over which CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE declares not to exercise any type of control, and does not assume any responsibility whatsoever for them.
eventos1: Our firm has a very active role in the academic and cultural sphere, actively participating in academic events such as national and international congresses, national and international conferences, seminars, working committees, workshops, academic workshops, masterclasses, webinars, and even artistic and cultural events.
eventos2: In each of them we usually occupy different roles, such as: organizers, co-organizers, speakers, coordinators, moderators, sponsors, or we simply attend as invited guests. In light of this, it is possible that our organization may also collect information that you voluntarily provide us, since through our website we offer you the opportunity to register to participate in these events or to access an online survey about your experience at them.
eventos3: When we collect this type of information, we will inform you of the reasons why we are requesting it and how that information will be used. It will be entirely up to you whether to provide it or not. Our organization will not use the information you provide for any purpose other than what is strictly necessary for the organization of the cultural, academic or artistic event to which you have been invited by our firm.
eventos4: For more information about our participation in events, you can access the "EVENTS" section on our website.
reserva1: The website of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE Abogados may contain hyperlinks or links to social networks, digital technology platforms or other third-party websites or applications over which CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE does not exercise any type of control. CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE does not control the contents, privacy policies, cookies and/or terms and conditions of such websites and assumes no responsibility for them.
tratamiento1: The data and information that CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE receives from users of its website will be duly safeguarded and may not be communicated, altered, transferred to third parties, modified or disclosed, except in those circumstances provided for and/or contemplated in this Privacy Policy, and in accordance with current applicable regulations.
tratamiento2: The processing of personal data carried out by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE shall be governed by the following internal guidelines and rules:
p1: 1. Application: This personal data privacy policy applies to all personal data processing carried out by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE through its website: www.cacciatoreabogados.com.ar, whose web domain is duly registered with NIC Argentina, establishing domicile for all legal and commercial purposes at Av. Piedrabuena 4858, 1st Floor, Office "C", Postal Code C1439, City of Buenos Aires, Argentine Republic.
p2: 2. Data collection means and platforms: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE may collect personal data from its clients, users, visitors to its website (registered or not), occasional and regular suppliers, sponsors, advertisers, business partners, and/or any other third party that interacts directly or indirectly with CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE through the contact forms established for such purposes on its website, and/or through the use of landline or mobile telephony and the Internet.
p2b: Each of these platforms has its own privacy policy, its own cookie policy and its own terms and conditions; documents and regulations over which CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE declares not to exercise any type of control, and does not assume any responsibility whatsoever for them.
p2c: Certain sections or options of our website may require users to provide certain personal data for their functionality, under the terms of the Personal Data Protection Law (Law No. 25.326). By entering their data, users give their full and proper consent for it to be collected and processed by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE, and the user also undertakes to provide true, reliable and accurate information, and to keep it updated at all times.
p3: 3. Purpose and object of data collection: The data that could be collected by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS would be those considered "indispensably necessary" to carry out the operational activity inherent to the legal professional activity that our firm develops, constantly striving for the correct processing and safeguarding of said data.
p3b: Eventually, we may analyze and use this information to find out which elements are most consulted or how the experience of our website users turns out, which in turn would allow us to identify ways to improve and adapt it to make it more effective. It is also possible that we could use this data for other specific purposes, for which we will inform you of the reasons at the time of data collection.
p4: 4. Expiration: Data will be destroyed or archived when they are no longer strictly necessary or relevant to the purposes of the object contemplated in the previous point of this data privacy policy.
p5: 5. Third Party Due Diligence: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE, in application of due diligence towards third parties, extends and applies these data privacy policies to the third parties with whom we interact directly or indirectly through the means and platforms contemplated in point (2) of these policies.
p5b: In this regard, CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE shall remain unrelated and indemnified with respect to illegal access and improper use of personal data by those aforementioned technological platforms contemplated in point two of these policies, represented by the subjects or entities that own them, which are external and unrelated to the structure of our firm.
p6: 6. Confidentiality: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE adopts the measures it considers necessary to guarantee the security and confidentiality of the personal data obtained in accordance with the provisions of these policies, in order to prevent alteration, loss, damage, impairment, unauthorized consultation or processing, and to detect deviations, intentional or not, of information, regardless of whether the risks come from human action or the technical means used.
p6b: All information used or communicated through emails between CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE and its clients or users of its website(s) shall be considered CONFIDENTIAL information, under the terms of article 318 of the Civil and Commercial Code of the Nation (Law 26.994), and shall be protected by professional secrecy and intended exclusively for its recipient(s). Any unauthorized use, disclosure of information, or partial or total copy of said message is strictly prohibited.
p6c: If a security incident is detected that poses a significant risk to the data subject, such event shall be immediately reported without delay to the NATIONAL DIRECTORATE FOR THE PROTECTION OF PERSONAL DATA OF THE AGENCY FOR ACCESS TO PUBLIC INFORMATION, regardless of the corrective or palliative measures implemented and/or to be implemented by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS.
p7: 7. Commitment not to transfer personal data: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS undertakes not to transfer the personal data collected from its clients, users and/or visitors to its website, declaring its full commitment not to transfer said data to other State agencies directly, except to the extent that the recipient requires it within the framework of its powers and competence; and the data processing carried out is compatible with its functions. The same applies to requirements from Argentine courts that are lawful provided they do not contradict the principles enshrined and protected by the professional secrecy that assists attorneys.
p7b: Under no circumstances shall data be transferred to private parties by CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS, who in turn shall not be responsible if the data is presumably in the public domain or public access, or if it is an implicit consideration linked to the control of public management, for which reason it is not possible to extend attribution factors of liability to CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS.
p8: 8. International Transfer of personal data: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE does not foresee the international transfer of personal data, all in compliance with the provisions of Law 25326, which prohibits the transfer of personal data of any type with countries or international or supranational organizations that do not provide adequate levels of protection.
p9: 9. Data processing on behalf of third parties (Including events): In the near or distant future, CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS may enter into agreements, conventions, pacts or contracts with third parties by virtue of the academic, cultural and/or professional development activities in which our firm usually participates in different roles.
p9b: For the eventual conclusion of this type of agreements or conventions, CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE will evaluate, classify and select third-party co-contractors ensuring that they enjoy recognized academic, cultural and/or professional prestige in the market.
p9c: Contracts and/or agreements that may eventually be entered into with third-party co-contractors may stipulate the object, scope, content, duration, nature and purpose of the data processing that the third party may eventually require, the type of personal data it requests, the categories of data subjects and the obligations and responsibilities of third-party co-contractors, being the full and total discretion of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE not to conclude or accept said contracts, agreements, pacts or accords if they threaten this data privacy policy or the Terms and Conditions of Use of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS.
p9d: We do not commercialize the personal data of our users and only share information internally with our team of associated professionals, although there will be certain special occasions when we will need to share information. However, before you send us any data or information, we will inform you of the reasons for which we will be requesting specific information and it will always be up to the users to provide it or not.
p9e: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE does not control, nor will it control the contents, privacy policies, cookies and/or terms and conditions of its co-contractors, for which reason it assumes and will not assume any responsibility for the actions or omissions of the latter.
p10: 10. Sensitive data: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS declares under oath that it does not collect and will not collect sensitive data from users of its website, nor will it store or process sensitive data of natural persons with whom it interacts through the means listed in point two (2) of this policy; understanding as sensitive data of a natural person all data concerning issues of racial and ethnic origin, political opinions, religious, philosophical or moral convictions, trade union membership and information relating to health or sexual life. CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS affirms that it does NOT collect and does NOT process sensitive data of persons.
p10b: If we really need to collect this type of data, we will ask you to give your express consent to the intended uses for that type of data.
p10c: Incidental collection: we may eventually collect this type of data incidentally when the user provides us with sensitive personal data not requested by our website, for which reason the user's express consent regarding the information that they eventually provide us without a request on our part will be apparent.
p11: 11. Links to social networks, technology platforms or other third-party websites: The website of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS may contain links to third-party sites, whose privacy policies are unrelated to our firm, for which reason we do not and will not have a duty to respond.
p11b: These links to third-party websites, technology platforms or social networks are provided solely for the convenience of the client, user or visitor of the CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS website and as such do not represent a direct or indirect endorsement by us of the content provided on such third-party portals or website platforms.
p11c: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS does not analyze, supervise, control, publish or edit the contents of third-party websites, or the social networks of third-party entities; and therefore does not ensure or respond for the truthfulness or legitimacy of the advertisements and digital content hosted on the websites, networks or third-party platforms.
p11d: If a user of the CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS website decides to access third-party websites linked to the CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS website out of their own interest, it shall be understood that they will have accessed them at their own risk and responsibility.
p11e: Third-party websites, platforms and social networks shall be subject to the terms and conditions applicable to them, including the privacy and/or cookie policies they may have, and if they do not have such terms and policies or protocols, the owner of the website, platform or social networks in question shall be responsible for damages caused, over which CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS shall have no responsibility whatsoever.
p12: 12. Advertising and announcements on the website: CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS may include third-party advertising and announcements on its website; however, said announcements may contain links to other third-party websites. Unless otherwise specified, CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS does not endorse or guarantee any product or service offered or advertised by these third parties and/or with respect to the content, accuracy or legitimacy of materials contained in or available through the link to any advertisement inserted on the CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS website.
p13: 13. Rights of data subjects / Dispute resolution: By virtue of the provisions of disposition no. 4 of the NATIONAL DIRECTORATE FOR THE PROTECTION OF PERSONAL DATA and arts. 27, inc. 3 of law 25.326 and 27, paragraph 3 of decree 1558/01, we inform you that you have the right to withdraw or block, total or partial, your name from our database, for which reason you may exercise your rights by sending us an email expressing your requirements or concerns to cacciatorehoracio@gmail.com or through the contact forms on our website www.cacciatoreabogados.com.ar
reserva2: The website of CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS may contain hyperlinks or links to social networks, digital technology platforms or other third-party websites or applications over which CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS does not exercise any type of control. CACCIATORE & CACCIATORE ABOGADOS does not control the contents, privacy policies, cookies and/or terms and conditions of said websites, nor does it assume or will it assume any responsibility for them.
contacto_final: If you have any questions or concerns regarding the content of our privacy policies, please do not hesitate to contact us by email: cacciatorehoracio@gmail.com
fecha: Last updated: May 2026 — Buenos Aires, Argentina.
